things.name: Krasnodar City Park
things.description: Galitsky Park, Russia's most spectacular privately-funded urban park opened in 2017; 23 hectares of stunning landscape architecture, sculptures, amphitheatre and tropical greenhouse

things.name: Krasnodar Regional Museum
things.description: Major museum of Kuban history and culture with rich collections on the Cossack heritage, Black Sea antiquities, WWII and the natural history of the region

things.name: Krasnodar Art Museum
things.description: Regional art gallery with impressive collections of Russian art from the 18th century to the present, including the remarkable bequest of collector Fyodor Kovalenko

things.name: Red Street (Krasnaya Street)
things.description: Krasnodar's elegant main pedestrian boulevard with 19th-century merchant buildings, pavement cafes and boutiques; the social spine of the city known as the Krasnodar Arbat

things.name: Krasnodar Zoo
things.description: One of Russia's best zoological parks with over 4,200 animals; the big cat collection and African savanna exhibit are highlights of this well-maintained city zoo

things.name: Cathedral of the Holy Trinity
things.description: Krasnodar's principal Russian Orthodox cathedral with gleaming white walls and blue-gold domes; rebuilt after Soviet-era demolition and consecrated in 2006

things.name: Kuban Cossack Quarter
things.description: Historic heart of the former Cossack stanitsa (village) with traditional architecture, the Kuban Cossack Army Museum and cultural centres preserving Cossack traditions

things.name: Abrau-Durso Winery (day trip)
things.description: Russia's oldest and most prestigious sparkling wine producer established in 1870; cellar tours, tastings and boat trips on the stunning crater lake in the Caucasus foothills

things.name: Theatre Square
things.description: Krasnodar's grand civic square with the Drama Theatre, Philharmonic Hall and fountains; the ceremonial centre of city life hosting festivals and seasonal events

things.name: Krasnodar Stadium
things.description: Modern 35,074-seat stadium opened in 2016 with a unique circular design; home of FK Krasnodar, billionaire-backed club who reached the UEFA Champions League group stage in 2020-21
